title: Project Overview
description: Start with the README and package manifest to understand that War Room is a Nuxt dashboard for making Hermes multi-profile delegation and Kanban work visible and directable.
languageLesson: Nuxt projects combine Vue UI files with server-side Nitro routes, so understanding both app/ and server/ is essential.

title: Application Shell and Main Screens
description: Inspect the app shell and route pages that frame the War Room, Missions, and Team views before diving into lower-level components.

title: Mission Control UI Components
description: Follow the mission and workstation components that render live delegation state, task cards, operative drill-downs, and command surfaces in the browser.

title: Client-Side State and Live Streams
description: Review the composables that poll Kanban state, subscribe to mission streams, transform task feeds, render markdown, and keep the UI reactive.
languageLesson: Vue Composition API composables centralize reusable reactive behavior; files named useX typically expose shared stateful logic.

title: Browser-to-Server API Boundary
description: Trace the Nitro API routes that the UI calls to create missions, dispatch Kanban work, list profiles and capabilities, and fetch runtime state.

title: Mission and Orchestration Core
description: Dive into the server utilities that persist mission state, emit live updates, run Hermes turns, handle locks, decompose work, and integrate with ACP/orchestrator execution.

title: Profiles, Roster, Skills, and Tooling
description: Inspect the support utilities that discover Hermes profiles, manage roster metadata, read/write profile configuration, and surface skills, tools, MCP servers, presets, and agent metadata.

title: Automation and Release Surface
description: Finish with repository-level automation: CI, release configuration, lint/typecheck setup, and workspace/runtime metadata that support building and shipping the dashboard.
